title: Operators
summary: Math operators add, subtract, multiply, and divide.
Comparison operators check if values are equal or bigger.
Logical operators combine true or false answers.

sections.text: Once you have values, you need to do things with them. Add two numbers, check if a score is high enough, decide if two things match. Operators are the small symbols that do this work.

sections.title: Math and Comparison
sections.text: Math operators work like school math, plus, minus, times, and divide. Comparison operators ask a question and answer true or false. Is this bigger than that, are these two equal. They are how your code makes decisions.

sections.title: Logical Operators
sections.text: Logical operators combine true or false values. And is true only when both sides are true. Or is true when at least one side is true. Not flips true to false and back. These let you build more careful conditions.

media.alt: And, or, and not combining true and false values

sections.title: A Kitchen Scale and a Checklist
sections.text: Math operators are like a scale, combining amounts. Comparison operators are like asking is this heavier than that, yes or no. Logical operators are like a checklist rule, serve the dish only if it is cooked and seasoned. Both must be ticked.

media.alt: A scale weighing, then a checklist with two boxes being ticked

sections.title: Using Operators
items: Pick the values you want to work with.
For math, use plus, minus, times, or divide.
To compare, ask a question that gives true or false.
To combine conditions, use and, or, and not.
Use the result to decide what your code does next.

sections.text: When checking if two things are equal, JavaScript has a strict version that also checks the type. Using strict equals avoids surprising bugs, so most developers always use it.

terms.definition: A symbol that does something with values.
Checking values, giving true or false.
Combines true or false values, like and and or.
One of the two boolean values.

questions.explanation: Comparison operators ask a question and answer true or false.

options: When at least one side is true
Only when both sides are true
questions.explanation: The and operator is true only when both sides are true.

options: It also checks the type, avoiding surprises
questions.explanation: Strict equals checks both value and type, which avoids surprising bugs.
